title: euxolus macrocarpus
intro: Euxolus macrocarpus, also known as the Chinese foxglove or yellow foxglove, is a flowering plant species native to China, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow or white flowers. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and is often used to treat coughs and sore throats. Euxolus macrocarpus is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, and can also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine preparations.
